Description: Assessment rules for Microsoft Entra ID identity security, Conditional Access, governance, and monitoring.

Rules.Description: Checks MFA registration coverage across member users using the authentication methods registration report or per-user method query
Verifies that an enabled Conditional Access policy blocks legacy authentication protocols (Exchange ActiveSync, Other clients)

Rules.SecureScoreMapping: Ensure there are at most 5 global admins
Rules.Description: Checks that the number of permanent Global Administrator assignments is between 2 and the configurable maximum (default 5)
Identifies user accounts flagged as 'atRisk' or 'confirmedCompromised' in Entra Identity Protection that have not been remediated (requires P2 license)
Verifies that Conditional Access policies are enabled. Flags if no CA policies are active and Security Defaults is also disabled

Rules.SecureScoreMapping: Do not allow users to grant consent to unmanaged applications
Rules.Description: Checks whether unrestricted user consent to OAuth applications is enabled (legacy consent policy), exposing the tenant to consent phishing
Reviews guest invitation settings to ensure only administrators and designated Guest Inviters can invite external users
Checks whether Self-Service Password Reset (SSPR) is enabled to reduce helpdesk load and social engineering risk from manual resets
Identifies enabled guest accounts that have not signed in within the configurable threshold (default 90 days)

Rules.CheckName: Entra ID Audit and Sign-in Log Accessibility
Rules.Description: Verifies that audit logs and sign-in logs are accessible and current, confirming log pipeline health
